title: Junior Research Analyst - survey based (primary research)
required_skills: Previous experience in primary research, insights or data analytics from media/advertising industry
Highly numerate with analytical mindset and genuine passion for research
Confident communicator able to present to internal and external stakeholders
Strong time management and project management skills
Attention to detail for testing survey links and reviewing raw survey data
matched_skills: German B1 (partial — role requires full bilingualism C1+)
Stakeholder presentation and communication skills (HSBC C-suite presentations, Hapag-Lloyd sprint reviews)
Data analysis and reporting (KPI analysis at HSBC, Hapag-Lloyd)
missing_skills: Primary research experience — no survey design, sample management, or quantitative research methodology background
Media/advertising industry experience — candidate has no media or advertising sector background
Statistical data processing: coding, weighting, tabulations
Brand lift research or digital tagging methodologies
German at C1/bilingual level — candidate has B1
core_responsibilities: Designing online surveys with support from senior researchers covering research objectives
Managing sample collection, setting representative quotas, quality checking raw survey data
Processing data in Decipher online survey platform; coding, weighting, creating tabulations
Charting reports focused on actionable insights
Presenting findings back to clients clearly and engagingly
Maintaining close daily communication with clients and stakeholders
recommended_emphasis: Do not apply — primary survey research experience is the core competency requirement and the candidate has none
Media/advertising industry background is explicitly required ('from within the media/advertising industry') — no match
German bilingualism is a must-have that the candidate's B1 does not meet
Analytical and presentation skills are genuinely strong but insufficient to overcome domain and language gaps
summary: Match score is 18.8 (poor_match). This role specifically requires primary survey research experience from the media/advertising industry, full German-English bilingualism, and familiarity with survey data processing (Decipher platform). The candidate has strong analytical and presentation skills but lacks all domain-specific requirements. The German language gap alone is a hard disqualifier given the bilingual requirement. Do not apply.
